customer_pain_radar.frequency: 4 instances — 2 named accounts ($4,200/mo each), 2 survey respondents
customer_pain_radar.workaround: Internal IT security exceptions filed; parallel vendor evaluations already underway before renewal

customer_pain_radar.frequency: Low frequency, high signal — 1 interview + enterprise ticket pattern
customer_pain_radar.workaround: 'The Real Dashboard' Google Sheet updated Mon/Wed, 8+ months running, shared with leadership as official status view
Parallel tracking spreadsheets maintained indefinitely — higher org scope, worse the tool works

customer_pain_radar.frequency: All 3 source types — confirmed backend bug
customer_pain_radar.workaround: Gmail filters archiving all system emails — critical signals (assignments, @mentions, deadlines) now invisible

customer_pain_radar.theme: Tools built for analysts, not users
customer_pain_radar.workaround: Team leads build shadow onboarding docs; non-technical users settle into read-only habits permanently
Separate company terminology doc pasted into prompts manually; non-technical users skip AI features entirely

competitive_threat_map.recent_move: AI Workspace + People Analytics Agents (Feb 2026, post $12.3B Thoma Bravo acquisition)
competitive_threat_map.rationale: PE-fueled, directly targeting Beacon's mid-market segment, price-aggressive. Dayforce can buy features, not context.

competitive_threat_map.recent_move: Sana 'superintelligence for work' (GA 2026) + Data Cloud zero-copy sharing
competitive_threat_map.rationale: Moving down-market aggressively. Beacon's wedge is standalone value with faster time-to-insight — Workday requires full HCM suite.

competitive_threat_map.recent_move: MCP for AI Agents (GA April 2026) — governed data infrastructure layer
competitive_threat_map.rationale: Enterprise-heavy, prices out of Beacon's segment. MCP play raises switching costs for enterprise accounts already on Visier.

competitive_threat_map.recent_move: Translytical Task Flows (GA 2026) — write data back to source from BI reports
competitive_threat_map.rationale: Directional confirmation that Bet 3 (Insight-to-Action) is where the market is heading. Not a direct threat today.

competitive_threat_map.recent_move: Rebrand to Data Studio + Conversational Analytics with Fast/Thinking mode NL query
competitive_threat_map.rationale: GCP-first threat. NL query is now table stakes. Beacon's AI needs org specificity to differentiate from generic NL.
Compliance-driven analytics wedge. Beacon should have a compliance analytics answer for regulated verticals.

priority_stack.action: Ship SAML SSO by May 31. No slip.
priority_stack.what: SAML SSO GA by hard date in Goal 3 (KR 3.1)
priority_stack.why_now: Two named accounts have June renewal conversations. SSO not GA = both accounts lost. Named dollar value attached.
priority_stack.okr: Goal 3, KR 3.1. Unlocks $193K in stalled expansion ARR across 23 accounts.
priority_stack.competitive_defense: Dayforce will price aggressively to take at-risk accounts. An SSO slip gives them a clean opening.
priority_stack.risk_if_missed: Two accounts churn in June. Every day of delay is a day Dayforce has to make contact.

priority_stack.action: Ship Org Context Engine to beta by Q3. This is the moat.
priority_stack.what: Org Context Engine (KR 2.1) — AI outputs referencing company-specific role names, team structures, workflows at ≥4.0 specificity
priority_stack.why_now: Dayforce launched AI Workspace in February. Workday shipped Sana. Both are generic. The 18-24 month window is running.
priority_stack.okr: Goal 2, KRs 2.1-2.3. Also drives KR 1.1 (WAU 34%→55%) by making AI usable beyond power users.
priority_stack.competitive_defense: No competitor can solve per-org context at their scale. This is Beacon's asymmetric bet before Dayforce approximates it.
priority_stack.risk_if_missed: Org Context slips to 2027 H1. NPS stays below 50. Referral flywheel doesn't turn. $32M ARR not achievable without a sales investment Beacon can't make.

priority_stack.action: Fix the notification bug. It's a silent engagement killer.
priority_stack.what: Resolve backend bug where notification preferences don't persist (TK-1203). Add granular controls: new assignments, overdue items, direct @mentions only.
priority_stack.why_now: Users creating Gmail filters that archive all system notifications — including critical ones. Daily engagement degrader with no roadmap owner.
priority_stack.okr: Indirect support for Goal 1 (WAU) and KR 1.4 (NPS 47→55). A user archiving all system emails is not an active user.
priority_stack.competitive_defense: Hygiene fix, not competitive. But a product that trains users to ignore it is hard to recover.
priority_stack.risk_if_missed: Daily active engagement continues to degrade silently. NPS stays flat. Utilization data at renewal doesn't support the price.

out_of_scope_fy2026: Spreadsheet elimination (Bet 3 is Q4 directional, not FY2026 completion)
